# Task Requirements Quality Checklist: Bounding-Box Conversion and Single-User Web GUI Ingestion

**Purpose**: Validate that `tasks.md` requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and traceable to feature requirements before ongoing implementation/review work.
**Created**: 2026-03-04
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all mandatory functional requirements (FR-001 through FR-021, including FR-003a/003b, FR-010a/010b, FR-015a, and FR-019a/019b) mapped to at least one explicit task ID? [Completeness, Spec §Functional Requirements, Tasks §Phases 3-7]
- [x] CHK002 Are FR-010a and FR-010b each represented by both implementation and test tasks rather than implementation-only coverage? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010a/FR-010b, Tasks §US1]
- [x] CHK003 Are FR-019a (contention behavior) and FR-019b (artifact compatibility) covered by distinct tasks with non-overlapping intent? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019a/FR-019b, Tasks §US2 and Phase 7]
- [x] CHK004 Are FR-015a protocol-warning and provenance obligations decomposed into both behavior tasks and validation tasks? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015a, Tasks §US3]
- [x] CHK005 Are all measurable outcomes SC-001 through SC-011 linked to at least one task or explicit traceability note? [Completeness, Spec §Success Criteria, Tasks §US1-US4 and Phase 7]
- [x] CHK006 Does `tasks.md` explicitly identify any intentionally deferred requirements so no scope is silently dropped? [Gap, Tasks §Notes]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Are task descriptions specific enough that two engineers would produce the same artifact paths and deliverables without extra interpretation? [Clarity, Tasks §Format]
- [x] CHK008 Is the "tests first (must fail before implementation)" rule defined with explicit evidence expectations for failing-first baselines? [Ambiguity, Tasks §Within Each User Story]
- [x] CHK009 Are brace-expanded multi-file tasks unambiguous about minimum required outputs per referenced file? [Clarity, Tasks §T022/T023/T043]
- [x] CHK010 Is "Foundation complete" defined with objective completion criteria beyond checkbox status? [Measurability, Tasks §Phase 2 Checkpoint]
- [x] CHK011 Are the quality-gate commands in T058 explicitly enumerated or linked in a way that prevents command-set drift? [Clarity, Tasks §T058 and checklists/implementation.md]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK012 Do phase dependency statements align with user-story dependency statements and incremental delivery ordering? [Consistency, Tasks §Dependencies & Execution Order and §Implementation Strategy]
- [x] CHK013 Are user-story priorities (P1/P2/P3) consistent with the stated MVP-first and parallel-team strategy? [Consistency, Tasks §Phases 3-6 and §Implementation Strategy]
- [x] CHK014 Are `[P]` markers consistently applied with the rule that parallel tasks must be dependency-safe and file-isolated? [Consistency, Tasks §Format and §Parallel Opportunities]
- [x] CHK015 Are required-test expectations applied consistently across all behavior-changing stories and governance workflows? [Consistency, Tasks §Tests and §US1-§US4]
- [x] CHK016 Do governance tasks for traceability and regression evidence align with review requirements in the feature spec and plan constitution gates? [Consistency, Spec §US4, Plan §Constitution Check, Tasks §US4]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 Are independent test statements for each user story measurable outcomes rather than implementation activities? [Acceptance Criteria, Tasks §US1-§US4 Independent Test]
- [x] CHK018 Are tasks sufficient to evaluate SC-002, SC-003, and SC-011 thresholds without introducing undocumented metrics later? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002/SC-003/SC-011, Tasks §T060/T061/T062]
- [x] CHK019 Are pass/fail requirements for policy gates (traceability and regression evidence) explicitly defined in task-level outputs? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007, Tasks §T048-T051]
- [x] CHK020 Can Phase 7 completion be objectively determined from documented outputs instead of subjective "hardening complete" interpretation? [Measurability, Tasks §Phase 7]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are primary, alternate, and exception CLI scenarios represented in tasks (success conversion, unmapped `error`, unmapped `drop`, and dry-run mismatch)? [Coverage, Spec §US1 Acceptance Scenarios, Tasks §US1]
- [x] CHK022 Are GUI failure and boundary scenarios (ambiguous inference blocking, localhost-only access, same-path contention outcome reporting) fully represented by tasks? [Coverage, Spec §US2 Acceptance Scenarios, Tasks §US2]
- [x] CHK023 Are import failure scenarios (inaccessible source, invalid artifacts, protocol-warning paths) covered by explicit tasks instead of implied prose? [Coverage, Spec §US3 Acceptance Scenarios, Tasks §US3]
- [x] CHK024 Are reviewer workflow scenarios (behavior change without spec linkage, bug fix without regression test) encoded as enforceable task outcomes? [Coverage, Spec §US4 Acceptance Scenarios, Tasks §US4]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Is each listed edge case mapped to at least one task or explicitly marked out-of-scope for this cycle? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Tasks §US1-US3]
- [x] CHK026 Is accidental converted-output writing during dry-run covered by both behavior and verification requirements? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases and FR-010, Tasks §T062/T063]
- [x] CHK027 Are malformed import artifacts and `file://` path-safety boundary cases decomposed enough to avoid hidden implementation assumptions? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases and FR-015a, Tasks §T041/T042/T047]
- [x] CHK028 Is deferred multi-user conflict scope clearly separated from required same-output-path contention behavior for v1? [Assumption, Spec §Edge Cases and §Assumptions, Tasks §US2]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK029 Are plan-level performance goals translated into measurable task requirements with explicit fixture scope? [Non-Functional, Plan §Performance Goals, Tasks §T056/T060/T061]
- [x] CHK030 Are determinism and auditability requirements represented by both implementation and verification-oriented tasks? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-009/FR-017/FR-018/FR-021, Tasks §US1/US2/Phase 7]
- [x] CHK031 Are schema compatibility requirements (current v1 minor and N-1) specified with clear acceptance boundaries? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-019b, Tasks §T059/T066]
- [x] CHK032 Are fail-closed expectations for ambiguous inference and import validation represented as explicit blocking requirements in tasks? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-005 and §External Data Sources, Tasks §US1/US3]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK033 Are assumptions about credentials, local workspace, and single-user scope translated into actionable task constraints or explicit non-goals? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Tasks §US2/US3]
- [x] CHK034 Are external dependency prerequisites (provider APIs, network access, fixture provenance) documented wherever missing prerequisites could invalidate task outcomes? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK035 Are documentation and contract update tasks synchronized with implementation tasks so reviewers can validate behavior without reverse-engineering code changes? [Dependency, Tasks §US4 and Phase 7, Plan §Phase 1 Design Outputs]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK036 Are terms like "equivalent output," "deterministic ordering," and "success-rate benchmark" defined with objective comparison rules in requirements artifacts? [Ambiguity, Spec §SC-003/SC-004, Tasks §US1/US2/Phase 7]
- [x] CHK037 Are there overlapping tasks whose boundaries are insufficiently distinct and could produce conflicting ownership or duplicate work? [Conflict, Tasks §US1-US3]
- [x] CHK038 Is there a defined cadence for keeping `tasks.md` synchronized after future spec/plan amendments? [Gap, Tasks §Notes and §US4]
